report_text: Navigation incident: Vessel Algoma Guardian experienced steering difficulty entering Western Channel in crosswind. Vessel corrected with tug assistance. No contact with channel markers. VTS Toronto monitored throughout.

Pollution observation: Small oil sheen 100m x 30m near Outer Harbour Terminal during morning patrol. No vessel in immediate area. Source traced to stormwater outfall from adjacent construction site. City of Toronto notified.
Accident report: Bulk carrier contacted berth fender at Terminal 51 during approach in gusting winds. Two fender panels compressed beyond limits. Minor hull scrape. Estimated repair cost $180,000.
recommended_actions: Improve coordination between commercial and recreational traffic

report_text: Near-miss: Outbound general cargo vessel and inbound laker had closest approach of 60m in Western Channel. Both within channel limits. Contributing factors: timing of harbour entrance and limited channel width.
recommended_actions: Enhanced vessel traffic management during crosswinds

report_text: Weather damage: Severe lake effect snow event deposited 30cm on terminal equipment. Two shore cranes had ice accumulation on cables. Port at 60% capacity for 24 hours during clearing operations.

Navigation report: Increasing sedimentation noted at Outer Harbour approach. Soundings show 0.5m reduction from charted depth. Draft advisory issued for vessels drawing more than 8m. Dredging request submitted.

Pollution prevention: Vessel Erie Navigator failed oily water separator test during inspection at Port Lands Terminal. Discharge at 38ppm exceeding 15ppm limit. Vessel detained under MARPOL. Repaired and released after 3 days.

Near-miss: Recreational sailboat crossed commercial fairway near Eastern Gap during summer weekend. Cargo vessel took avoiding action. Min distance 60m. Toronto Police Marine Unit issued safety warning.
recommended_actions: Enhanced vessel traffic management during crosswinds

report_text: Accident: Mooring line failure on vessel at Commissioners Street Wharf during cargo operations. Line snapback in clear zone. Vessel shifted 1.5m. Operations suspended 3 hours. Line replacement required.
recommended_actions: Enhanced vessel traffic management during crosswinds

report_text: Weather report: Dense fog on Lake Ontario reduced visibility to 30m in Toronto Harbour for 5 hours. Three inbound vessels anchored outside harbour. Two departures delayed. Total delay: 42 vessel-hours.
recommended_actions: Enhanced vessel traffic management during crosswinds

report_text: Hazard report: Submerged object detected during routine channel survey near Cherry Street Terminal. Identified as discarded construction piling from Port Lands redevelopment. Marked and removal scheduled.

Near-miss: Container fell from crane spreader at 3m height during lift at Port Lands Terminal. Container struck dock, no personnel in area. Spreader latch mechanism found worn. Fleet inspection ordered.
recommended_actions: Review berthing procedures for gusty conditions

report_text: Weather: Microburst event produced 90km/h gust at Terminal 51. All cranes auto-shutdown. One container in transit safely grounded by operator. No damage. 30-minute all-clear procedure followed.
recommended_actions: Review berthing procedures for gusty conditions

report_text: Accident: Harbour patrol boat struck floating debris near Eastern Channel causing hull damage. Crew safe. Boat towed to marine base. Debris identified as dock section from recent storm damage.

Navigation: Vessel exceeded 6-knot speed limit in inner harbour near residential waterfront. Wake caused complaints from marina operators at Harbourfront. Port Authority issued violation notice and $25,000 fine.
Pollution: Garbage discharge observed from vessel at anchorage during early morning. Plastics and food waste documented. MARPOL Annex V violation. Administrative penalty of $12,000 issued.

Near-miss: Rail car coupling failure on port spur track near Port Lands Terminal. Two cars separated during switching. No derailment. Track blocked 4 hours. Metrolinx rail safety inspection triggered.

Weather impact: Spring ice breakup on Lake Ontario created unusual conditions near harbour entrance. Large ice floes blocked Western Channel for 6 hours. Four vessels delayed. Ice-breaking requested from Canadian Coast Guard.
Accident: Container truck rollover on Cherry Street ramp in wet conditions. Driver minor injuries. Container secure on chassis. Ramp closed 3 hours. Drainage assessment of ramp surface initiated.

Navigation report: Vessel navigating at night reported partial failure of port-side navigation lights near Toronto Island. Temporary fix applied. Full repair at next port of call. VTS provided enhanced monitoring.
